intent:
PREMISE CONTRACT (story 2, v2.5 bootstrap slot 2)
  one_sentence_event: After breakfast, mother and I leave the house together, walk down the bright road from home to the corner shop, and mother buys a small apple (one of several items, also bread) with money; the apple ends up in mother's bag.
  change_of_state: at s0 the apple is in the shop (offstage / unowned by us); at the closer the apple is in mother's bag (owned by us, located inside our bag). Money flows out, apple flows in: a real transaction, not pure observation.
  scene_novelty_claim: scene_class=walk_to_shop. Story 1 was home_morning; no overlap.
  anchor_novelty_claim: anchor is りんご (apple). Story 1 used お茶/台所; no overlap.
  closer_shape_claim: closer is the existential settling beat 「りんごは母の袋にあります。」(subject-possessive-noun-に-verb). NOT the banned 「[X]は[Y]を[Z]に持ちます」 from story 1's closer; NOT a noun-pile.
  why_not_filler:
    - 母 (mother) earns her slot: she is the one who actually performs the buying transaction; the child is companion, not actor.
    - 私 (the child) earns the slot through accompaniment (companion-と) and as observer; without 私 the と-and intro has no anchor.
    - the apple (anchor) earns its slot via the object-causality test: it changes owner (shop → mother) AND changes location (shop → bag). Three appearances: s4 (on the shelf, small), s6 (bought), s7 (settled).
    - the bread (パン, reinforce slot) earns its slot via the も-also reinforcement: it is parallel to the apple on the shop shelf, motivating the も-also and giving the buy a real choice (mother selects the apple, not the bread).
    - the road (道, mint) earns its slot as the transit between scene_class endpoints; named once with an adjective (明るい, reinforcing W00001) so it is not a decorative noun.
  obligations_absorbed:
    - must_reinforce (grammar): G022_i_adj (s3 明るい, s4 小さい), G015_no_possessive (s7 母の袋), G001_wa_topic (s2/s3/s6/s7), G003_desu (s3), G004_ni_location (s4/s5/s7), G021_aru_iru (s4 あります, s5 あります, s7 あります), G009_mo_also (s5 パンも). All seven must_reinforce grammar items satisfied (the gauntlet's pedagogical_sanity step blocked the prior draft for missing G009_mo_also; this revision adds 「パンもあります。」at s5 to land it natively in the shop scene).
    - must_reinforce (vocab): 明るい s3, 朝 s1, 家 s1+s2, 母 s1/s2/s6/s7, 私 s1/s2, 小さい s4, 有る s4/s5/s7, 歩く s2, パン s5. Word-reinforce coverage is widened versus the prior draft by trading the clerk slot for the bread slot.
    - must_introduce (grammar): seed_plan items N5_to_and (s1/s2 母と私, intros G010_to_and), N5_kara_from (s2 家から, intros G006_kara_from), N5_de_means (s6 お金で, intros G017_de_means). Three new grammar points, within ladder 3-5.
    - must_mint (vocab): seed plan items 一緒 (s1), 出る (s1), 店 (s2), 道 (s3), りんご (s4), お金 (s6), 買う (s6), 袋 (s7) = 8 mints, within ladder 6-10. Excludes 行く and 店員 (deferred — see amendments).
    - All obligations are NATIVE FITS (Step C.3 path a) — the walk-to-shop scene generates と/から/で and the mint set naturally; the も-also reinforcement is satisfied by the parallel shelf-bread, not by a teleported character.
  FORBIDDEN ZONES (carried from story 1, 2026-04-29):
    - scene_class home_morning (used by story 1) — not used.
    - anchor_object tea_cup/台所 (used by story 1) — not used.
    - closer-shape 「[X]は[Y]を[Z]に持ちます」 — not used; closer is existential (はNにあります).
  AMENDMENTS DOCUMENTED:
    - Seed-plan vocab 行きます dropped from story 2 (deferred). The walk-to-shop scene uses 歩く+へ for directional motion; adding 行く would be redundant or push mint count over 10. Defer to a story whose scene requires a destination not reachable on foot (e.g. station/train).
    - Seed-plan vocab 店員 (clerk) dropped from story 2 (deferred). The original draft included a clerk to motivate the transaction, but the gauntlet's pedagogical_sanity step required reinforcing G009_mo_also, and the cleanest fit was a parallel-shelf-item sentence (パンもあります) rather than a third character. The transaction works fine without an on-stage clerk — mother simply buys the apple. Defer 店員 to a later shop-scene story where mo is no longer in the must_reinforce window.
  LEXICAL_OVERRIDE (added 2026-04-29 evening, after the lexical-difficulty cap landed):
    - 袋 (bag, JLPT N3, nf28) is above the bootstrap-tier cap of N5/nf06 but is genuinely scene-grounding for the change_of_state contract: at s0 the apple is offstage in the shop, at the closer it is in mother's bag. Without 袋 the closing locative beat (「りんごは母の袋にあります。」) collapses into something more abstract ('with mother', 'at home') that breaks the visceral, object-tracked transaction the story is built around. ナイフ-style alternatives don't exist for 'bag' — かばん is N5 but means 'satchel/handbag' and would mis-cue the reader to expect a school setting. Override accepted; 1 of MAX_OVERRIDES_PER_STORY=2 used.

NATURALNESS REWRITE 2026-04-30: s4 added 店に prefix to the dangling locative パンもあります → 店にパンもあります; s5 dropped redundant お金で and shifted to past tense 母はお金でりんごを買います → 母はりんごを買いました (introduces G013_mashita_past). Net: -1 mint (お金 dropped), de_means intro shifts to story 3 via 包丁で.

sentences.jp: 朝、母と私は一緒に家を出ます。
sentences.en: In the morning, mother and I leave the house together.

sentences.jp: 母と私は家から店へ歩きます。
sentences.en: Mother and I walk from the house to the shop.

sentences.jp: 店に小さいりんごがあります。
sentences.en: At the shop there is a small apple.
At the shop there is also bread.

sentences.jp: 母はりんごを買いました。
りんごは母の袋にあります。
sentences.en: The apple is in mother's bag.
